chapter goal: Which transformations preserve a measurement, and how do reflections generate them?
computational checks: cross-ratio invariance under a projective reparametrization
join/meet incidence for generated homogeneous points
mode-specific conic or hyperbolic numeric check when relevant
gaps: Proof-heavy material in Chapter 21 is represented by measurement diagrams, determinant/cross-ratio checks, or small parameter experiments rather than copied text.

visual sequence.expected_observation: The absolute conic decides which transformations preserve angle or distance.
visual sequence.learner_inspection_target: For Chapter 21, inspect oriented measurement comparison by identifying which objects instantiate measurements versus oriented measurements.
visual sequence.library: numpy, matplotlib, local projective/conic/Cayley-Klein helpers, and self-contained HTML
visual sequence.validation_target: absolute-conic pole/polar checks and distance sample are finite
